Fnrn Poltrtcal Pna,crtcr,s C onnuISSIoN
P.O. Box lt07 . "128 J Street 'Sacrrnretrto. CA 95811-0tt07
(9 l6) .122-5660 ' Firx (916) j22-01t86
January 19,2001
Jim fu ssmiller, Treasurer
San Bernardino County Taxpayers PAC
28019 Millar Street
Highland, C A 923 46 -27 9 |
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-264
Dear Mr. Rissmiller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the San
B ernardino C ounty T axp ayers PAC regarding the small-contributor-committee
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").'
QUESTION
The San Bernardino County Taxpayers PAC has maintained its status as a small
contributor committee under Proposition 208. Must the committee amend its statement
of organization to reflect that it ii a small contributor committee under Proposition 34?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Before contributing under the higher applicable contribution limit, the San
Bernardino County Taxpayers PAC must amend its statement of organization to reflect
that it is a small contributor committee under Proposition 34.
Ai\ALYSIS
On Novemb er 7,2000,the voters approved Proposition 34, which, among other
things, imposes contribution limits on candidates for elective state office. (Section
85301.) Under Proposition 34, small contributor committees may contribute more to
candidates than other persons may, with the exception of political parties. (Section
8s302.)
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-00-264
Page No. 2
Section 85203 defines a small contributor committee as a committee that meets all
of the following criteria: (1) the committee has been in existence for at least six months;
(2) the committee receives contributions from 100 or more persons; (3) no one person has
contributed to the committee more than two hundred dollars ($200) per calendar year;
and (4) the committee makes contributions to five or more candidates.
Emergency Regulation 18503 (copy enclosed) clarifies the definition of small
contributor committee. In addition, the regulation provides that a committee, which
qualifies as a small contributor committee, must amend its statement of organization
before contributing at the higher level. (Section 18503(b).) The amended statement of
organization must reflect that the committee is a small contributor committce and the date
on which it qualified as such. For committees that qualified before January 1, 2001, the
date of qualification is January 1,2001.
The Commission has noticed Regulation 18503 for permanent adoption in
March.2 I have enclosed a copy of the noti." for your review. Also enclosed is the
revised Statement of Organization Form 410, which you may use to amend the
committee's registration. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincereiy,
Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel
By: Julia Bilaver, Staff Counsel
Legal Division
JB je
Enclosures
I : \Advicel.hs\002 64.doc
2 As noted in the noticed regulation, the version recommended for permanent adoption differs
slightly from the emergency regulation adopted in January. The changes are grammatical and do not affect
the advice in this letter.

CONCLUSION: Yes. Before contributing under the higher applicable contribution limit, the San

that it is a small contributor committee under Proposition 34.
